data.title: PROG Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of $1.24 beats by $0.46, revenue of $742.7M beats by $10M
data.description:
• PROG Holdings press release (PRG): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.24 beats by $0.46.

data.title: Bidding war for jet fuel pushes European airlines to the brink: CNBC
data.description:
Europe is locked in a fierce global “bidding war” for jet fuel as supplies from the Middle East remain cut off, forcing the continent to “fight for every cargo” in what analysts are calling a stress test for the airline industry, CNBC reported.

“While we can import more, and we are, from the U.S. and Nigeria, we have to fight for every cargo that’s going to come,” Benedict George, head of European product pricing at Argus, told CNBC. “We have to fight against Singapore, against Australia, and the price…just goes higher and higher.”

The scale of the supply deficit is staggering, according to analysts at Societe Generale. Europe’s average daily demand of about 1.6 million barrels of jet fuel relies on imports for roughly 500,000 barrels, three-quarters of which traditionally arrived from the Middle East before the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed following the U.S.-Iran conflict that began Feb. 28.

With a current deficit of approximately 175,000 barrels per day, SocGen analysts warned that “paying more for energy is manageable; not having it is existential.”

The United States has stepped in as a critical alternative supplier, but the competition is intense.

As CNBC reported, U.S. jet fuel exports surged to a record 442,000 barrels a day in early April, with Europe's share jumping from a pre-war range of 30,000–60,000 barrels daily to roughly 200,000 barrels.

"U.S. consumers and U.S. airlines are competing against Europeans and against Singapore and so on for that American jet fuel," George told CNBC, noting that unlike some Asian nations, the U.S. has not moved to restrict exports.

Major carriers are deploying hedging strategies to cushion the blow, though analysts caution these may not be enough if physical fuel availability, not just price, becomes the primary constraint.

Lufthansa has cancelled approximately 20,000 flights to conserve more than 40,000 metric tons of jet fuel, and has hedged roughly 80% of its 2026 fuel requirements at pre-crisis levels, according to the report. SocGen analysts flagged to CNBC that flight cancellations driven by unavailable fuel would be a "far more disruptive outcome" than elevated prices alone.

The financial pain is already flowing through to passengers. Air France-KLM has raised economy fares on long-haul return flights by 100 euros, and by 70 euros for U.S., Canada, and Mexico routes, citing the "sharp and sudden" rise in fuel costs, a company spokesperson told CNBC.

"For the upcoming months we are continuously analyzing and monitoring the situation, as well as various scenarios that could potentially impact our operations, including as a result of the blockade in the Strait of Hormuz," the spokesperson added.

data.title: Federal National Mortgage Association GAAP EPS of $0.01, revenue of $7.28B beats by $30M
data.description:
• Federal National Mortgage Association press release (FNMA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.01.
  • Revenue of $7.28B (+2.8% Y/Y) beats by $30M.

data.title: SoFi Technologies in Charts: Q1 growth driven by lending surge as members and products continue to scale
Guanajuato Silver Company Ltd. GAAP EPS of -$0.04, revenue of $22.7M
data.description:
• Guanajuato Silver Company Ltd. press release (GSVR:CA): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.04.
  • Revenue of $22.7M.

  • $10.3 million accrued liability provision for legal costs and losses related to the potential unfavorable outcome in the lawsuit with NucTech Mexico, S.A. de C.V. announced on July 22, 2025 and updated on December 4, 2025; this lawsuit remains under appeal and will continue to be vigorously defended by the Company.

  • A non-cash $6.8M derivative accounting loss generated by the gold-loan with Ocean Partners. This non-cash loss accounted for over 25% of the total net loss; the loan structure continues to act as a synthetic hedge to the Company's gold production.

  • $2.8M for other legal accruals.

  • A $1.2M write-down of the Cata mill, which was put on care & maintenance in December 2025.

  • A $2.4M write-off for legacy Value Added Tax that was determined to be non-recoverable.

data.title: Sierra Madre Gold and Silver Ltd. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Sierra Madre Gold and Silver Ltd. press release (SM:CA): Q4
  • Revenue of $8.32M.
  • Cost of sales was $4.58 million for Q4 2025 or $33.70 per AgEq ounce sold as compared to $3.82 million for Q3 2025, or $24.85 per AgEq ounce sold, $4.07 million for Q2 2025, or $23.45 per AgEq ounce sold, and $3.60 million for Q1 2025, or $21.84 per AgEq ounce sold.
  • Gross Profit was $3.72 million for Q4 2025, as compared to $2.10 million for Q3 2025, $1.69 million for Q2 2025 and $1.36 million for Q1 2025.

data.title: Verisk raises dividend by 11% to $0.50
data.description:
• Verisk (VRSK) declares $0.50/share quarterly dividend, 11.1% increase from prior dividend of $0.45.
  • Forward yield 1.13%
  • Payable June 30; for shareholders of record June 15; ex-div June 15.
  • See VRSK Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Navient Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20 beats by $0.04, revenue of $126M misses by $6.98M
data.description:
• Navient press release (NAVI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $126M (-19.2% Y/Y) misses by $6.98M.

data.title: Vita Coco beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Vita Coco press release (COCO): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.50 beats by $0.18.
  • Revenue of $180M (+37.4% Y/Y) beats by $31.64M.
  • Non-GAAP Adjusted EBITDA1 was $39 million, an increase of $16 million.
  • Vita Coco Coconut Water net sales grew 42%.

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Full Year Outlook

    The Company is increasing its full year 2026 guidance as follows:

    • Net sales expected to be between $720 million and $735 million  (prior $680 million and $700 million) vs. $696.78Mconsensus, driven by mid-to-high teens growth of Vita Coco Coconut Water and improvements in Private Label trends from new and regained business. (Prior guidance was between $680 million and $700 million).
    • Gross margin expected to be approximately 38% with benefit relative to prior year from lower cost of goods due to a reduction in tariffs and higher pricing partially offset by adverse product mix, inflationary impacts, and increased branded promotion and incentives. (Unchanged from prior guidance).
    • SG&A expenses expected to increase high single digits versus 2025. (Prior guidance was mid to high single digit growth).
    • Adjusted EBITDA1 expected to be in the range of $132 million to $138 million. (Prior guidance was between $122 million and $128 million).

data.title: The Chefs' Warehouse beats Q1 street views, guides FY revenue above consensus
data.description:
• The Chefs' Warehouse press release (CHEF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.40 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $1.06B (+11.5% Y/Y) beats by $50M.

  • 2026 Guidance

    • Net sales in the range of $4.35 billion to $4.45 billion (VS. consensus of $4.41B)
    • Gross profit to be between $1.053 billion and $1.076 billion and
    • Adjusted EBITDA1 to be between $276 million and $286 million.
